item.text: I’ll read the finding and inspect the referenced code paths first, then patch only the local source needed to remove the vulnerability.

item.aggregated_output:
Project: AsyncHttpClient/async-http-client
Vulnerability type: Information Exposure (CWE-200)

Where the vulnerability is (file -> vulnerable line ranges, with enclosing function):

  client/src/main/java/org/asynchttpclient/netty/handler/intercept/Redirect30xInterceptor.java
    - lines 41-46
    - lines 113-119   in: public boolean exitAfterHandlingRedirect(Channel channel, NettyResponseFuture<?>
    - lines 209-216   in: private static HttpHeaders propagatedHeaders(Request request, Realm realm, boole

/work
pom.xml
mvnw.cmd

LICENSE.txt
README.md

item.text: The finding points at redirect handling, especially header propagation. I’m going to inspect that interceptor and nearby tests around redirect credential behavior before editing.

The current code already has some credential stripping logic, so I’m checking whether the base-origin comparison is precise enough and whether generated auth can still reappear through request/future realms.

The sharp remaining issue is `Proxy-Authorization`: it is a proxy credential and should not be copied into the redirected origin request even when the redirect stays on the same origin. I’m going to add a focused regression test for that and make the interceptor always drop it while preserving normal `Authorization` behavior for same-origin redirects.

I’m editing the redirect header propagation now: `Authorization` will still be conditional, but `Proxy-Authorization` will be treated as non-propagatable redirect state. I’ll add the same-origin regression test where the bug is easiest to observe.
The production change is small and scoped. I’m adding the regression assertion so a future redirect refactor cannot reintroduce proxy credential forwarding.
The code and regression test are in place. I’m running the redirect credential tests now, then I’ll inspect the diff for any accidental churn.

item.text: `git diff --check` reported no whitespace issues. The remaining changed files are only the interceptor and its focused redirect test.

Remediated the redirect credential exposure.

Changed [Redirect30xInterceptor.java](/work/client/src/main/java/org/asynchttpclient/netty/handler/intercept/Redirect30xInterceptor.java:203) so redirected requests always remove `Proxy-Authorization`, while preserving existing conditional behavior for `Authorization`.

Added a regression test in [StripAuthorizationOnRedirectHttpTest.java](/work/client/src/test/java/org/asynchttpclient/StripAuthorizationOnRedirectHttpTest.java:99) to verify `Proxy-Authorization` is not propagated even on same-origin redirects.

Verification:
`git diff --check` passed.

I could not run the Maven tests because both `./mvnw` and system `mvn` failed resolving Maven Central artifacts due `Unknown host repo.maven.apache.org`.
